sections.paragraph:
fut. יִטֹּל-

(1) to take up, to lift. (Syr. ܢܛܰܠ to be heavy, from the idea of carrying. Cogn. roots are תָּלַל, תָּלָה, τλάω, τλῆμι, tollo, and those which are formed from them, as tolero.) Isa 40:15, כַּדַּק יִטֹּול “as a particle of dust (which any one) takes up.” [“He takes up the isles as dust.” Thes.]

(2) Followed by עַל to lay upon some one. 2Sa 24:12, שָׁלשׁ אָנֹכִי נֹוטֵל עָלֶיךָ “I lay upon thee three things;” (in the parallel place, 1Ch 21:10, there is נֹוטֶה. In the same sense is said נָתַן לִפְנֵי Jerem. 21:8 ). Lam 3:28, כִּי נָטַלּ לֳָיו “because (God) has laid upon him the load of calamity.”

Piel, i.q. Kal No. 1, Isa 63:9.

Derivatives נָטִיל, נֵטֶל.
